---
title: "Quarto 7 — Projects and Safe Sharing"
subtitle: "Know what the project controls, then choose the right sharing unit"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
---

## Goals for this 20-minute block

This is an **instructor-led demonstration and discussion**. By the end, you should be able to:

- Recognize what a Quarto project controls.
- Distinguish a website from a standalone report.
- Explain why a website must normally be shared as a folder or deployed as a site.
- Choose an institutionally approved sharing method for the sensitivity of the content.

::: {.callout-important}
## Keep the course website read-only
Do not edit the workshop's root `_quarto.yml` during class. It controls every page in the training website. Your editable report remains in `student_work/my_report.qmd`, which belongs to a separate, small Quarto project.
:::

## 1. What a Quarto project controls

A Quarto project is a directory with a `_quarto.yml` file at its root. The file can define shared formats, execution settings, navigation, resources, and which documents belong to the project.

```text
my-project/
├── _quarto.yml
├── index.qmd
├── report.qmd
├── references.bib
├── styles.scss
└── _site/               # rendered website output, when applicable
```

This workshop contains two deliberately different projects:

| Project | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Workshop root | `website` | Read-only lessons and navigation |
| `student_work/` | `default` | A small learner workspace whose files render independently |

The separation matters. Rendering or previewing a page inside a website project can involve project-level work beyond the page you are viewing. A small learner project makes errors easier to locate and prevents one unfinished lesson from blocking another.

### Common project types

| Type | Use it for |
|---|---|
| `default` | Reports or notebooks that share settings but render independently |
| `website` | A multi-page site with navigation and search |
| `book` | Ordered chapters with unified navigation and bibliography |
| `manuscript` | A scholarly article connected to computational notebooks |

The complete project documentation is at <https://quarto.org/docs/projects/quarto-projects.html>.

## 2. Themes: observe, do not edit the course site

The instructor will briefly show the root `_quarto.yml` and `styles.scss`. The site combines a built-in Bootstrap theme with local SCSS:

```yaml
format:
  html:
    theme:
      light: [cosmo, styles.scss]
```

A standalone report can use a simpler setting:

```yaml
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
```

For ordinary CSS rules, use the `css:` key:

```yaml
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    css: custom.css
```

For Bootstrap variable overrides, place an SCSS file in the theme list and include a Quarto SCSS layer boundary:

```scss
/*-- scss:defaults --*/
$primary: #205C40;

/*-- scss:rules --*/
h1, h2, h3 {
  color: $primary;
}
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Wednesday's priority
A report that renders reproducibly is more valuable than a heavily customized report that does not. Treat theming as a post-workshop refinement.
:::

## 3. Conditional output across formats

An HTML widget such as `DT::datatable()` is not portable to every output format. Provide a static fallback:

```{r}
#| eval: false

if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  DT::datatable(my_data)
} else {
  knitr::kable(my_data)
}
```

Useful checks include:

```r
knitr::is_html_output()
knitr::is_latex_output()
knitr::pandoc_to("docx")
```

## 4. A website and a standalone HTML report are different sharing units

### Website project

The normal output of this workshop website is a directory such as `_site/`. Its pages can depend on shared style sheets, scripts, images, and search assets. Share or deploy the **complete output directory**, not one HTML page copied out of it.

```bash
quarto render
```

### Standalone report

For a single HTML report that is easier to email or archive as one file, enable embedded resources in that report:

```yaml
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
```

This embeds most supporting resources into the HTML file. When MathJax content also needs to work offline, review Quarto's `self-contained-math` option and test the file on a disconnected computer.

See <https://quarto.org/docs/output-formats/html-publishing.html#embed-resources>.

## 5. Choose a sharing destination based on the content

| Content | Appropriate approach |
|---|---|
| Public-release training material | Approved public website, Quarto Pub, or GitHub Pages |
| Internal material | Approved shared drive, internal web server, or institutionally managed publishing service |
| Controlled, sensitive, protected, or unpublished data | Only an environment and destination approved for that data |

::: {.callout-warning}
## Public publishing is public
Do not publish personally identifiable information, protected health information, controlled data, sensitive agency information, or unpublished results to Quarto Pub, GitHub Pages, or another public service. A private external repository is not automatically approved for agency data. Follow your organization's records, privacy, security, and release-review requirements.
:::

The instructor may show the syntax without running it:

```bash
# Public-release material only, after required review
quarto publish quarto-pub
quarto publish gh-pages
```

Quarto's publishing overview distinguishes public services from organizational platforms such as Posit Connect: <https://quarto.org/docs/publishing/>.

## Discussion: choose the sharing unit

For each case, name both the **output** and the **destination**:

1. A public workshop handout with no sensitive information.
2. An internal multi-page methods site.
3. A single report for an approved internal collaborator.
4. An analysis containing controlled participant-level data.

A defensible answer states not only *how* to publish, but also *why that destination is authorized*.

## End-of-day sequence

You have already completed the cumulative capstone. Finish with:

1. Render `student_work/my_report.qmd` from a clean session.
2. Save or export your learner workspace using the method provided by the instructor.
3. Complete the exit ticket in [`../next_steps.qmd`](../next_steps.qmd).
